name: isis (partner in mummification)
shortDescription: Isis is a major goddess in ancient Egyptian religion whose worship spread throughout the Greco-Roman world. She was revered as the ideal mother and wife, patron of nature and magic, and friend of slaves, sinners, artisans, and the downtrodden.
description: Isis, whose Egyptian name was Aset, is a prominent goddess in ancient Egyptian religion. She was initially worshipped as a funerary deity, but evolved to become a powerful protector, healer, and magician. Isis is best known as the wife of Osiris and the mother of Horus, playing a vital role in the Osiris myth. After Osiris was murdered and dismembered by Set, Isis tirelessly searched for his body parts, reassembled them, and resurrected him, demonstrating her immense power and devotion. Her role in mummification is crucial, as she, with the help of other deities like Anubis, performed the first mummification on Osiris, setting the precedent for all subsequent mummification rituals. She protected the resurrected Osiris and later protected their son, Horus, from Set. Isis embodies motherhood, magic, and healing. Her cult spread far beyond Egypt, becoming popular in the Greco-Roman world, where she was often associated with other goddesses like Demeter and Venus. Her image frequently depicts her with wings, a throne headdress, or suckling Horus. Isis's enduring appeal lies in her portrayal as a powerful, compassionate, and resourceful goddess who offers hope and protection to her worshippers.

sources.title: Egyptian Ideas of the Future Life
sources.author: E.A. Wallis Budge
